title: `-f` argument causes `-j` to be ineffective for build
body:
We encounter the following error using Verilator 5.032 2025-01-01 rev v5.032.

Running the following command, where `VLT_FLAGS` contains multiple `+incdir+`, `+define+` and Verilog file arguments, results in Verilator correctly employing 128 threads during build:
```
verilator $(VLT_FLAGS) -j 128 -o ../bin/testharness.vlt --cc --exe --build --top-module testharness
```
On the contrary, when moving the `VLT_FLAGS` arguments to a file and including the same arguments using the `-f` flag, the build is done sequentially, one compilation unit at a time:
```
verilator -f <path_to_flist_file> -j 128 -o ../bin/testharness.vlt --cc --exe --build --top-module testharness
```
